text: In 1905, a 26-year-old patent clerk, a guy whose academic career had completely stalled,
scene: Doodle-Einstein, young, sitting at an old wooden patent-office desk piled with paperwork, a wall calendar reading 1905, simple flat office. Big black caps 1905. (Doodle-Einstein is a crude stick figure with a round white head, wild messy white hair sticking out, a bushy white mustache, dot eyes, thin line body. He is clearly a different character from any plain bald stick figure.)

text: sat down between stamping other people's paperwork and casually rewrote the universe.
scene: Doodle-Einstein at the patent desk stamping a paper with one hand while casually scribbling glowing equations on a scrap with the other. Rewriting the universe between stamps. (Doodle-Einstein is a crude stick figure with a round white head, wild messy white hair sticking out, a bushy white mustache, dot eyes, thin line body. He is clearly a different character from any plain bald stick figure.)

text: Atoms, light, space and time itself, one man, one year, in his spare time.
scene: Doodle-Einstein gesturing at three floating glowing icons: a tiny atom, a beam of light, and a bending clock. Big black caps ONE MAN. ONE YEAR. (Doodle-Einstein is a crude stick figure with a round white head, wild messy white hair sticking out, a bushy white mustache, dot eyes, thin line body. He is clearly a different character from any plain bald stick figure.)

text: And here's the strange part, it feels like nobody does that anymore.
scene: Stan standing in the present scratching his head, a faded ghostly outline of Einstein dissolving away beside him. Big black caps NOBODY DOES THAT ANYMORE. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: Try to name a living Einstein, working today, and most of us like Stan here,
scene: Stan straining to think with an empty thought bubble above him hand-lettered LIVING GENIUS?, simple flat background. Coming up blank. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: just come up blank. So, what happened? Did humanity peak and start coasting?
scene: Stan shrugging with both hands up beside a big red question mark, big black caps DID WE PEAK? (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: The real answer is a lot weirder, and it is not the one you are bracing for.
scene: A big glowing red question mark with a winding arrow curving away from an obvious straight path, big black caps WEIRDER THAN YOU THINK.

text: And up close, what he actually pulled off in that one year is even more absurd.
scene: Doodle-Einstein cracking his knuckles at the patent desk, the paperwork pushed aside, big black caps ONE YEAR. (Doodle-Einstein is a crude stick figure with a round white head, wild messy white hair sticking out, a bushy white mustache, dot eyes, thin line body. He is clearly a different character from any plain bald stick figure.)

text: He proved that atoms are real, something scientists had argued about for centuries.
scene: A cluster of simple round atoms with a big green check mark, doodle-Einstein pointing at them, big black caps ATOMS ARE REAL. (Doodle-Einstein is a crude stick figure with a round white head, wild messy white hair sticking out, a bushy white mustache, dot eyes, thin line body. He is clearly a different character from any plain bald stick figure.)

text: He explained that light comes in tiny particles, the idea that would later win him the Nobel Prize.
scene: A beam of light breaking into tiny glowing particles, a gold Nobel medal beside it, big black caps LIGHT + THE NOBEL.

text: And then, almost as a side project, he rewrote space and time themselves with special relativity,
scene: A clock and a ruler both bending and warping around doodle-Einstein, big black caps SPACE + TIME. (Doodle-Einstein is a crude stick figure with a round white head, wild messy white hair sticking out, a bushy white mustache, dot eyes, thin line body. He is clearly a different character from any plain bald stick figure.)

text: and dropped in a little equation you have definitely heard of.
scene: Doodle-Einstein casually dropping a small folded note labeled with a glowing equation into the frame, big black caps A LITTLE EQUATION. (Doodle-Einstein is a crude stick figure with a round white head, wild messy white hair sticking out, a bushy white mustache, dot eyes, thin line body. He is clearly a different character from any plain bald stick figure.)

text: E equals MC squared. Remember, his doctoral thesis had just been rejected.
scene: Huge glowing hand-lettered E=mc2 filling the frame, a small REJECTED stamp on a thesis paper in the corner.

text: He was a nobody doing this between stamping patents.
scene: Doodle-Einstein shrugging modestly at the patent desk between two stamps, hand-lettered A NOBODY above him. (Doodle-Einstein is a crude stick figure with a round white head, wild messy white hair sticking out, a bushy white mustache, dot eyes, thin line body. He is clearly a different character from any plain bald stick figure.)

text: One man, one year, off the side of his desk.
scene: The patent desk with one corner glowing where the physics happened, big black caps OFF THE SIDE OF HIS DESK.

text: And before you file that under abstract old-timey science,
scene: An old dusty book labeled OLD-TIMEY SCIENCE with a red X over the label, big black caps NOT JUST HISTORY.

text: understand that it is running your life right now.
scene: Stan holding a glowing smartphone, surprised, big black caps RUNNING YOUR LIFE NOW. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: The GPS in your pocket depends on his relativity.
scene: A phone with a glowing blue GPS dot on a map, a dotted line linking it back to a small doodle-Einstein portrait, big black caps GPS. (Doodle-Einstein is a crude stick figure with a round white head, wild messy white hair sticking out, a bushy white mustache, dot eyes, thin line body. He is clearly a different character from any plain bald stick figure.)

text: Without the correction Einstein's math provides,
scene: Doodle-Einstein's glowing math equations holding up the phone's map like scaffolding, big black caps HIS MATH. (Doodle-Einstein is a crude stick figure with a round white head, wild messy white hair sticking out, a bushy white mustache, dot eyes, thin line body. He is clearly a different character from any plain bald stick figure.)

text: your phone's location would drift off by about two kilometers every single day.
scene: A phone map with the blue dot drifting far off the road, a red arrow showing the drift, big black caps OFF BY 2 KM A DAY.

text: The blue dot that finds you on a map exists because a bored patent clerk got curious about light.
scene: The glowing blue you-are-here dot connected by a long dotted line back to doodle-Einstein daydreaming at the patent desk, big black caps THE BLUE DOT. (Doodle-Einstein is a crude stick figure with a round white head, wild messy white hair sticking out, a bushy white mustache, dot eyes, thin line body. He is clearly a different character from any plain bald stick figure.)

text: So, yes. Where are the Einsteins is a completely fair thing to ask.
scene: Stan with arms crossed nodding beside a big bold question, big black caps A FAIR QUESTION. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: But here's the first crack in the question.
scene: A magnifying glass over the question revealing a small crack running through it, big black caps THE FIRST CRACK.

text: The image in your head, the lone genius,
scene: A dramatic poster of a lone stick-figure hero in a spotlight on a pedestal, big black caps THE LONE GENIUS.

text: one brilliant mind alone in a room cracking the universe,
scene: The lone hero alone in a bare room cracking a glowing universe egg, dramatic spotlight, simple flat scene.

text: was always partly a myth.
scene: The same hero poster with a big red stamp across it, big black caps PARTLY A MYTH.

text: Even Einstein was not really alone.
scene: Doodle-Einstein looking over his shoulder at a crowd of other faint scientists behind him, big black caps NOT REALLY ALONE. (Doodle-Einstein is a crude stick figure with a round white head, wild messy white hair sticking out, a bushy white mustache, dot eyes, thin line body. He is clearly a different character from any plain bald stick figure.)

text: He was building on the work of others,
scene: Doodle-Einstein standing on top of a tall stack of books by earlier scientists, big black caps BUILT ON OTHERS. (Doodle-Einstein is a crude stick figure with a round white head, wild messy white hair sticking out, a bushy white mustache, dot eyes, thin line body. He is clearly a different character from any plain bald stick figure.)

text: arguing ideas with friends and his wife,
scene: Doodle-Einstein at a small table arguing happily with two other stick figures, papers flying, ideas sparking. (Doodle-Einstein is a crude stick figure with a round white head, wild messy white hair sticking out, a bushy white mustache, dot eyes, thin line body. He is clearly a different character from any plain bald stick figure.)

text: standing on a whole pile of physics that came before him.
scene: Doodle-Einstein standing on a huge pile of physics books and papers reaching up, big black caps A PILE OF PHYSICS. (Doodle-Einstein is a crude stick figure with a round white head, wild messy white hair sticking out, a bushy white mustache, dot eyes, thin line body. He is clearly a different character from any plain bald stick figure.)

text: We just love a single hero,
scene: A big glowing heart around a single hero figure, big black caps WE LOVE ONE HERO.

text: so we flatten the messy, crowded truth into one face on a poster.
scene: A messy crowded scene of many scientists being squeezed through a funnel into one clean face on a poster, big black caps FLATTENED.

text: Genius has never actually been a solo act.
scene: The words SOLO ACT with a big red X through them, big black caps NEVER A SOLO ACT.

text: We just tell the story that way.
scene: A storybook open to a page showing the single hero, big black caps JUST THE STORY.

text: And here is the real reason it feels so different today.
scene: A red arrow pointing forward, big black caps THE REAL REASON.

text: In 1905, the frontier of physics was close.
scene: A small mountain with a frontier flag planted low on its slope, a 1905 calendar beside it, big black caps THE FRONTIER WAS CLOSE.

text: Close enough that one sharp young man could sprint to the edge of human knowledge
scene: Young doodle-Einstein sprinting easily up the small slope toward the flag at the edge of knowledge, motion lines. (Doodle-Einstein is a crude stick figure with a round white head, wild messy white hair sticking out, a bushy white mustache, dot eyes, thin line body. He is clearly a different character from any plain bald stick figure.)

text: in his spare time and plant a flag.
scene: Doodle-Einstein planting a glowing flag at the low frontier, casual and happy, big black caps PLANTED A FLAG. (Doodle-Einstein is a crude stick figure with a round white head, wild messy white hair sticking out, a bushy white mustache, dot eyes, thin line body. He is clearly a different character from any plain bald stick figure.)

text: Today, that edge is incredibly far away.
scene: A huge towering mountain now, the frontier flag tiny and impossibly high at the peak, big black caps FAR AWAY NOW.

text: To even reach the frontier of a field now,
scene: A tiny modern stick figure at the very bottom of the huge mountain looking up at the distant frontier, simple flat.

text: just to get to the place where you could add something new
scene: A long winding dotted path up the mountainside marked with little graduation caps along the way, big black caps JUST TO ADD SOMETHING NEW.

text: takes decades of study.
scene: A calendar flipping through decades beside the tiny climber, big black caps DECADES OF STUDY.

text: Because we know so much more than we did.
scene: A glowing pile of accumulated knowledge books growing taller, big black caps WE KNOW SO MUCH MORE.

text: Scientists have a name for this.
scene: A scientist stick figure pointing at a chalkboard, big black caps THERE'S A NAME FOR IT.

text: The burden of knowledge.
scene: A chalkboard with huge hand-lettering, big black caps THE BURDEN OF KNOWLEDGE.

text: Every discovery we make pushes the starting line of the next one further out.
scene: A starting line being pushed further and further back by each new glowing discovery, red arrows, big black caps THE START MOVES.

text: The mountain did not get smaller.
scene: The huge mountain unchanged with a green check, big black caps SAME MOUNTAIN.

text: The hike just to reach base camp got enormous.
scene: A base camp tent halfway up the huge mountain with a long long trail just to reach it, big black caps BASE CAMP GOT FAR.

text: So the work itself changed shape.
scene: A glowing blob of work morphing from one shape into another, big black caps THE WORK CHANGED SHAPE.

text: The big problems left are tangled and gigantic.
scene: A giant tangled knot of a problem towering over a tiny stick figure, big black caps TANGLED AND GIGANTIC.

text: Too big for any one skull to hold.
scene: One small stick-figure head with a huge glowing problem hopelessly overflowing out of it, big black caps TOO BIG FOR ONE SKULL.

text: So science became teams.
scene: A team of many stick figures in lab coats joining hands around one glowing problem, big black caps SCIENCE BECAME TEAMS.

text: The breakthroughs are still happening constantly,
scene: Fireworks of small glowing breakthrough sparks going off across a flat skyline, big black caps STILL HAPPENING.

text: but now a single discovery might have 100 names on it.
scene: A single glowing discovery with a long list of 100 tiny names hanging off it like a tail, big black caps 100 NAMES.

text: The Einstein of today is not missing.
scene: A modern scientist stick figure with a faint Einstein-style glow standing mid-crowd, big black caps NOT MISSING.

text: He or she is one of 200 authors on a paper you will never read,
scene: A dense paper covered in 200 tiny crammed author names, one quietly highlighted, big black caps ONE OF 200.

text: and you will not learn a single one of those names.
scene: The same paper fading away unread, big black caps NAMES YOU'LL NEVER LEARN.

text: We did not lose the geniuses.
scene: A crowd of faintly glowing genius stick figures with a green check, big black caps STILL HERE.

text: We lost the one face to pin them on.
scene: An empty picture frame on a wall where one face used to go, big black caps NO ONE FACE.

text: Which leads to the real mystery underneath all of it.
scene: A deeper layer opening under the question like a trapdoor, big black caps THE REAL MYSTERY.

text: What even makes an Einstein in the first place?
scene: Doodle-Einstein drawn as a simple recipe card with a question mark, big black caps WHAT MAKES ONE? (Doodle-Einstein is a crude stick figure with a round white head, wild messy white hair sticking out, a bushy white mustache, dot eyes, thin line body. He is clearly a different character from any plain bald stick figure.)

text: And the honest answer from the science is that it was never just a magic brain.
scene: A glowing brain with a red X over a magic wand beside it, big black caps NOT JUST A MAGIC BRAIN.

text: Yes, some of it is born.
scene: A baby stick figure with a small glowing spark in its head, big black caps SOME IS BORN.

text: Raw intelligence is partly genetic.
scene: A simple DNA helix with a glowing segment, big black caps PARTLY GENETIC.

text: But the bigger half is conditions.
scene: A balance scale tipping heavily toward a side labeled CONDITIONS over a side labeled BRAIN, big black caps THE BIGGER HALF.

text: Being in the right field at the exact right moment,
scene: A calendar and a map pin glowing together, big black caps RIGHT FIELD, RIGHT MOMENT.

text: while it is still young enough to crack.
scene: A young green field with a small lock just starting to open, big black caps YOUNG ENOUGH TO CRACK.

text: Having the freedom and the empty hours to just think, remember,
scene: An empty armchair beside a window with long free hours drifting by as little clocks, big black caps FREEDOM TO THINK.

text: Einstein had a quiet, boring job with time to daydream,
scene: Doodle-Einstein leaning back at the quiet patent desk daydreaming, little thought bubbles of light beams above him, big black caps TIME TO DAYDREAM. (Doodle-Einstein is a crude stick figure with a round white head, wild messy white hair sticking out, a bushy white mustache, dot eyes, thin line body. He is clearly a different character from any plain bald stick figure.)

text: a streak of obsession, and a great deal of luck.
scene: A glowing flame labeled OBSESSION beside a pair of dice, big black caps OBSESSION + LUCK.

text: Move Einstein a hundred years in either direction,
scene: Doodle-Einstein sliding along a timeline arrow a hundred years in each direction, fading at both ends. (Doodle-Einstein is a crude stick figure with a round white head, wild messy white hair sticking out, a bushy white mustache, dot eyes, thin line body. He is clearly a different character from any plain bald stick figure.)

text: and maybe nobody ever hears his name at all.
scene: A faded empty portrait frame where Einstein's face would be, big black caps MAYBE NO ONE KNOWS HIM.

text: So here's the quiet truth.
scene: A calm soft glow over a simple flat scene, big black caps THE QUIET TRUTH.

text: We did not stop producing geniuses.
scene: A factory-style conveyor of little glowing genius sparks still rolling along with a green check, big black caps STILL PRODUCING.

text: People did not get dumber.
scene: A crowd of stick figures with a big red X over the words GOT DUMBER, big black caps PEOPLE DIDN'T GET DUMBER.

text: Two things changed.
scene: Two glowing shapes side by side, big black caps TWO THINGS CHANGED.

text: The shape of genius shifted from a lone hero into a hidden crowd.
scene: A single hero silhouette morphing into a glowing crowd of many small figures, big black caps HERO TO HIDDEN CROWD.

text: And the conditions that once let a single person rewrite everything got rare.
scene: The patent-office desk with its quiet glow becoming rare, fading like a museum piece, big black caps THE CONDITIONS GOT RARE.

text: Not because we got worse,
scene: A crowd with a green check beside it, big black caps NOT WORSE.

text: but because we already know so much that no one can hold it all anymore.
scene: An overflowing library of everything we know towering over one small head, big black caps TOO MUCH TO HOLD.

text: There are almost certainly people alive right now.
scene: A modern city crowd scene with a few figures faintly glowing among everyone, big black caps ALIVE RIGHT NOW. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: Every bit as brilliant as Einstein.
scene: One glowing figure in the crowd matching a small Einstein silhouette beside them, equals sign, big black caps JUST AS BRILLIANT.

text: You will simply never know their names.
scene: Glowing figures with blank name tags, big black caps YOU'LL NEVER KNOW THEIR NAMES.

text: They are standing in the middle of a team.
scene: A tight team huddle of stick figures with one glowing member in the middle, big black caps MIDDLE OF A TEAM.

text: A mile out at a frontier you cannot even see from here.
scene: A far distant frontier line on the horizon beyond rolling hills, tiny glowing team out there, Stan squinting from the foreground unable to see it, big black caps A FRONTIER YOU CAN'T SEE. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: So maybe that is the real answer to where the Einsteins went.
scene: Stan asking the big question one more time beside a glowing map, big black caps SO WHERE DID THEY GO? (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: Nowhere. They are just spread out now.
scene: The map zooming out to show the geniuses spread out everywhere as little glowing dots, big black caps NOWHERE. EVERYWHERE.

text: The next leap that changes everything probably will not come from one lonely mind in a patent office.
scene: The old patent office desk with a red X, one lonely mind crossed out, big black caps NOT ONE LONELY MIND.

text: It will come from a thousand minds and a machine.
scene: A thousand tiny stick figures plus a glowing computer all linked together, big black caps A THOUSAND MINDS AND A MACHINE.

text: Working on something most of us will never understand.
scene: A huge glowing abstract discovery shape too complex to read, tiny figures working on it, big black caps BEYOND US.

text: And no statue will ever be built for it.
scene: An empty pedestal with no statue on it, a small plaque with a question mark, big black caps NO STATUE.

text: The age of the lone genius is ending.
scene: A sunset behind the lone-hero statue silhouette, big black caps THE AGE IS ENDING.

text: Not because we ran out of geniuses.
scene: The conveyor of glowing genius sparks still rolling, a green check, big black caps NOT OUT OF GENIUSES.

text: But because the universe got harder to surprise.
scene: The universe drawn as a huge cosmic puzzle sphere with most pieces filled in, big black caps HARDER TO SURPRISE.

text: And we finally got big enough problems that we need each other to solve them.
scene: Many stick figures all gripping one giant glowing problem together and lifting it, big black caps WE NEED EACH OTHER.

text: So the next time you try to name a living Einstein and come up empty,
scene: Stan with the empty LIVING GENIUS? thought bubble again, but calm now, big black caps WHEN YOU COME UP EMPTY. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: do not read it as humanity slowing down.
scene: A red X over a downward graph labeled HUMANITY SLOWING DOWN, big black caps NOT SLOWING DOWN.

text: You are looking at what genius actually looks like now.
scene: Stan looking at a glowing crowd-shaped constellation, big black caps WHAT GENIUS LOOKS LIKE NOW. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: Quieter, shared, nameless, and honestly kind of more impressive.
scene: Three small hand-lettered words floating gently over the glowing crowd: QUIETER. SHARED. NAMELESS.

text: There was a time when one person was enough to move the whole world.
scene: A single old-timey figure moving a tiny globe alone, faded like an old photo, big black caps ONE PERSON WAS ENOUGH.

text: Now it takes all of us at once.
scene: A huge crowd of stick figures all together lifting the whole glowing world at once, big black caps ALL OF US AT ONCE.

text: And that might be the most human thing we have ever done.
scene: Stan looking up calm and awed at a starry night sky where the stars form a faint crowd of tiny figures, peaceful and hopeful, big black caps THE MOST HUMAN THING. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)
